LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT GOLDEN GATE DRYWALL, INC'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT/OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: THE MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED BECAUSE DESPITE SHIFTING OF THE BURDEN TO PLAINTIFF, THE MCCOLLUM AND AY DECLARATIONS CONTAIN ADMISSIBLE TESTIMONY CREATING A TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT. MCCOLLUM SAW DEFENDANT EMPLOYEE PERFORM DRYWALL WORK WITHIN SEVEN FEET OF DECEDENT (PARAGRAPHS 6, 7), AY'S DECLARATION (PARAGRAPHS 10, 11) CONTAINS EXPERT OPINION. DEFENDANT'S EVIDENTIARY OBJECTIONS NOS. 1,2,3,4,5,6 & 7 ARE OVERRULED. PLAINTIFF'S EVIDENTIARY OBJECTION TO THE MEDEIROS DECLARATION IS OVERRULED; PARTS OF IT MAY BE INADMISSIBLE, BUT PLAINTIFF UTTERLY FAILS TO OBJECT TO THESE SPECIFIC STATEMENTS. THE SO-CALLED D'AMICO RULE DOES NOT CONTROL HERE; MCCOLLUM DEPOSITION WAS TAKEN IN A DIFFERENT CASE SEVEN YEARS AGO AND LACKS SPECIFIC EXAMINATION ABOUT DECEDENT. THE SO-CALLED DISOLA RULE DOES NOT CONTROL HERE. STATEMENTS OF DEFENDANT'S EMPLOYEES TO MCCOLLUM CONSTITUTE ADMISSIONS. MOREOVER, HE SIMPLY DESCRIBES HIS OBSERVATION OF THE NAME ON VEHICLES. THE COURT DOES GRANT SUMMARY ADJUDICATION AS TO PLAINTIFF'S SECOND, THIRD AND SIXTH CAUSES OF ACTION. THE COURT ADDS: DEFENDANT'S REQUEST FOR JUDICIAL NOTICE IS GRANTED.
